fields.name: score
fields.description:
Integer from 0-4 (useful for implementing a strength bar):
0 too guessable: risky password. (guesses < 10^3)
1 very guessable: protection from throttled online attacks. (guesses < 10^6)
2 somewhat guessable: protection from unthrottled online attacks. (guesses < 10^8)
3 safely unguessable: moderate protection from offline slow-hash scenario. (guesses < 10^10)
4 very unguessable: strong protection from offline slow-hash scenario. (guesses >= 10^10)

types.name: Sync
types.description:
A Sync subscribes a CDE file to a Speckle model. Executions (runs) of a Sync
are represented by the SyncItem type; `latestItem` and `history` expose them.
`latestItemContext` and `modelIngestion` are convenience proxies for the
latest execution — prefer reading via `latestItem` for new code.

fields.name: active
fields.description:
Whether this sync is active. When false, new executions are not triggered
on file changes. Toggled by pause/resume mutations.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Static CDE config (region, hubId, lineageUrn, etc.)

fields.name: history
fields.description: Paginated history of sync executions (newest first)

fields.name: latestItem
fields.description: Latest sync execution (same row surfaced via proxy fields above)

fields.name: latestItemContext
fields.description: Version-specific context from the latest sync execution

fields.name: modelIngestion
fields.description:
Model ingestion record for the latest sync execution, if any.
Exposes queued/processing/success/failed/cancelled status with progress.

types.name: SyncItem
types.description:
A single sync execution — one run that downloads a file version from the CDE
and ingests it into Speckle. Executions accumulate per Sync and form its history.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Version-specific context captured at execution time (fileAuthorName, versionNumber, etc.)

fields.name: modelIngestion
fields.description:
Model ingestion record for this execution, if any. Use this for the
authoritative execution state — the sync-item row itself no longer
carries a status.

fields.name: migrateLegacy
fields.description:
Promote a legacy ACC sync (still in `acc_sync_items`) into the new `syncs`
schema. Surfaced as a user-facing button so people don't have to wait for
the next ACC webhook for trigger-now / pause / edit to work. The lazy
migration that fires on real webhooks does the same job; this is just the
explicit version. Syncs already in the new schema return as-is.

fields.name: setActive
fields.description:
Pause or resume a sync. Paused syncs skip polling/webhook-driven executions
and reject `update(..., triggerNow: true)`. In-flight executions finish.

fields.description: Versions within the date range, ordered by createdAt DESC.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Opaque cursor for loading older data. Null when no more data is available.

fields.name: projectGroups
fields.description: Projects with their versions, ordered by most recent version DESC.

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description: Either this or userId must be filled

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: Defaults to the contributor role, if not specified

inputFields.name: seatType
inputFields.description:
The workspace seat type to assign to the user upon accepting the invite
(if user is a workspace member already, the seat type will be updated)

inputFields.name: serverRole
inputFields.description: Can only be specified if guest mode is on or if the user is an admin

inputFields.name: userId
inputFields.description: Either this or email must be filled

inputFields.name: workspaceRole
inputFields.description: Only taken into account, if project belongs to a workspace. Defaults to guest access.

fields.name: children
fields.description: Child labels belonging to this group

fields.name: hexColor
fields.description: Null when this row is a group (no color)

fields.name: parent
fields.description: Parent group label, null for top-level labels and groups

inputFields.name: limit
inputFields.description:
Maximum 100
Default: 25

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Filter by name

inputFields.name: sortBy
inputFields.description:
Specify the field to sort by.
Default: lastUsedAt

inputFields.name: sortDirection
inputFields.description:
Specify the sort direction.
Default: desc

fields.name: moveToRegion
fields.description:
Schedule a job that will:
- Move all regional data to target region
- Update project region key
- TODO: Eventually delete data in previous region

inputFields.name: includeArchived
inputFields.description: Include archived projects in results. Only respected for workspace admins; silently ignored for non-admins.

inputFields.name: labelIds
inputFields.description: Filter projects by label ids (OR logic — returns projects that have any of the given labels)

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Filter out projects by name

inputFields.name: withProjectRoleOnly
inputFields.description: Only return workspace projects that the active user has an explicit project role in

fields.name: project
fields.description: Project entity, null if project was deleted

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project ID

fields.name: type
fields.description: Message type

fields.name: workspaceId
fields.description: Workspace ID

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: Leave role null to revoke access entirely

fields.name: provider
fields.description: If null, the workspace does not have SSO configured

fields.name: currentQuantity
fields.description:
Total number of add-on packs currently purchased for the workspace (0 if none).
Each pack grants a fixed amount of extra projects and versions.

fields.name: assigned
fields.description: Total number of seats in use by workspace users

fields.name: available
fields.description: Total number of seats purchased and available in the current subscription cycle

fields.name: approveAccess
fields.description:
Approve a pending support access request. Only workspace admins can approve.
Activates the session and notifies the requesting server admin.

fields.name: requestAccess
fields.description:
Request access to a workspace for support purposes. Only server admins can request access.
Creates a pending session and notifies workspace admins for approval.

fields.name: revokeAccess
fields.description:
Revoke/stop support access. Works on both pending (denial) and active (revocation) sessions.
Can be called by the requesting server admin or a workspace admin.

types.name: WorkspaceSupportSession
types.description:
A support session grants a server admin temporary access to a workspace for support purposes.
Sessions are requested by server admins and must be approved by workspace admins.
The session is tied to the admin user (not a specific token), so any auth token the admin
uses will have support access for the workspace while the session is active.

fields.name: adminUser
fields.description: The server admin who requested/holds this support session

fields.name: approvedAt
fields.description: When the session was approved by a workspace admin. Null if still pending.

fields.name: approvedBy
fields.description: The workspace admin who approved the session. Null if still pending.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: When the session request was created

fields.name: revokedAt
fields.description: When the session was revoked/denied. Null if not revoked.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current status of the session

fields.name: validUntil
fields.description:
When the session expires. Null means no expiration.
A session with a past validUntil is considered expired regardless of stored status.

fields.name: workspace
fields.description: The workspace this session grants access to

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: Filter by session status(es). Returns all statuses if not specified.

types.name: WorkspaceSupportSessionStatus
types.description:
Support session status lifecycle:
pending → active → revoked/expired

enumValues.name: active
enumValues.description: Session has been approved and support access is active

enumValues.name: expired
enumValues.description: Session has passed its validUntil timestamp

enumValues.name: pending
enumValues.description: Session has been requested by a server admin, awaiting workspace admin approval

enumValues.name: revoked
enumValues.description: Session was manually stopped/revoked by either party (covers both denial and revocation)

fields.name: session
fields.description: The affected support session

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of update that occurred

fields.name: workspace
fields.description: The workspace this session belongs to

inputFields.description: Limit team members to provided role(s)

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search for team members by name or email

inputFields.description: Logo image as base64-encoded string

fields.name: id
fields.description: Workspace ID

fields.name: workspace
fields.description: Workspace itself

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

enumValues.name: DIRECTIVE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a directive definition.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: hasScope
directives.description:
Ensure that if there is a token, the token  has the specified scope allowed for it
It does not ensure that the token exists, for that, use @hasServerRole

directives.name: hasScopes
directives.description:
Ensure that if there is a token, the token has all of the specified scopes allowed for it
It does not ensure that the token exists, for that, use @hasServerRole

directives.name: hasServerRole
directives.description: Ensure that the user has the specified SERVER role (e.g. server user, admin etc.)

directives.name: hasStreamRole
directives.description:
Ensure that the user has the specified STREAM role for a target stream (e.g. owner)

Note: Only supported on Stream type fields!

directives.name: hasWorkspaceRole
directives.description:
Ensure that the active user has the specified Workspace role
Note: Only supported on Workspace type fields

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: internalOnly
directives.description:
Restrict field access to internal (first-party) Speckle applications only.
Third-party app tokens, personal access tokens, and unauthenticated requests
are denied. Fields marked with this directive may change without notice.

directives.name: isOwner
directives.description:
Ensure that the authenticated user owns the object whose properties
are being accessed

Note: Only supported when added onto the fields that are directly defined on the
owned object.

Note 2: Only supports the following types currently: User

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
